pattern: Because rearrangement is free, the signs decouple from positions: the largest squares take the added slots and the smallest take the subtracted ones.
hints: Squaring removes the sign, so only the magnitudes' squares matter, and there are a fixed number of plus and minus slots.
There are ceil(n/2) added terms and floor(n/2) subtracted terms; you freely assign each square to a slot.
Sort the squares descending, add the first ceil(n/2) of them, and subtract the rest.

edge_inputs.description: two elements, one added one subtracted
negatives have large squares and must go to added slots

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randrange(-40000, 40001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
